title: Second-act support phase
summary: After the front-half comparison and memory work closed cleanly, lane 2 should support the stronger second act. This wave adds a compact high-country comparison and close-look pack, then deepens memory payoff for those same second-act anchors through existing notebook-facing surfaces.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the second-act comparison and close-look handoff.
execution_order.why_now: The inland branch now needs stronger authored teaching anchors rather than more front-half breadth.

execution_order.goal: Implement second-act comparison and close-look support.
execution_order.why_now: Treeline, tundra, and the forest vertical family can now carry more distinct microhabitat teaching.

execution_order.goal: Review second-act comparison and close-look support.
execution_order.why_now: These surfaces need to stay compact and science-safe.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the second-act memory payoff handoff.
execution_order.why_now: The new second-act anchors should also leave a better notebook memory trace.
The inland and vertical spaces should feel remembered without opening another archive shell.
Sketchbook or note growth must stay calm and handheld-safe.

suggested_notes.moss-campion: Dense cushion bloom on cold ground.
suggested_notes.tree-lungwort: Leafy lichen on cool damp bark.

guardrails: Reuse only existing comparison, close-look, sketchbook, and ecosystem-note surfaces.
Do not add station, route-board, or world-map logic.
Keep the pack narrow and second-act focused.
Prefer habitat-role contrast and remembered places over raw totals.
Keep `main-212` limited to `moss-campion` comparison support plus `tree-lungwort` close-look support; do not widen the allowlists further in the same pass.
Use `reindeer-lichen` only as supporting tundra note context if needed, not as a second new comparison or close-look target.
Keep `seep-stone` and `seep-moss-mat` out of this wave; they already own other second-act support seams.
